<content>
1: # ox_data_object_manager — Data Dictionary & Object Manager
2: 
3: **Crate:** `ox_data_object_manager` (sub-crate of `ox_data_object`)
4: **Type:** library
5: 
6: Adds a metadata layer on top of `GenericDataObject`. The data dictionary describes the
7: logical structure of objects and their physical mapping to datasource containers. The
8: `DataObjectManager` uses this metadata to load and save objects across one or more
9: datasources with automatic field remapping. Validation is a separate addon
10: (see [spec/validation.md](validation.md)).
11: 
12: ---
13: 
14: ## Data Dictionary
15: 
16: ### DataDictionary
17: 
18: The root metadata container. Serializes to/from JSON for file-based persistence.
19: 
20: ```rust
21: pub struct DataDictionary {
22:     pub containers: HashMap<String, DataStoreContainer>,  // keyed by container id
23:     pub objects: HashMap<String, DataObjectDefinition>,   // keyed by object id
24: }
25: ```
26: 
27: | Method | Description |
28: |--------|-------------|
29: | `new()` | Empty dictionary |
30: | `add_container(c)` | Insert container by id |
31: | `add_object(o)` | Insert object definition by id |
32: | `merge_container(c)` | Update fields of existing container; insert if new |
33: | `save_to_file(path)` | Serialize to JSON |
34: | `load_from_file(path)` | Deserialize from JSON |
35: 
36: ### DataStoreContainer
37: 
38: Describes a physical storage unit — a table, file, API endpoint, key, etc.
39: 
40: ```rust
41: pub struct DataStoreContainer {
42:     pub id: String,
43:     pub datasource_id: String,      // driver name in PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY
44:     pub name: String,               // physical name: table, file path, etc.
45:     pub container_type: String,     // "table" | "view" | "file" | "key"
46:     pub fields: Vec<DataStoreField>,
47:     pub metadata: HashMap<String, String>,
48: }
49: ```
50: 
51: ### DataStoreField
52: 
53: ```rust
54: pub struct DataStoreField {
55:     pub name: String,
56:     pub data_type: ValueType,
57:     pub parameters: HashMap<String, String>,
58:     pub description: Option<String>,
59: }
60: ```
61: 
62: ---
63: 
64: ## Object Definition
65: 
66: ### DataObjectDefinition
67: 
68: ```rust
69: pub struct DataObjectDefinition {
70:     pub id: String,
71:     pub name: String,
72:     pub description: Option<String>,
73:     pub attributes: Vec<DataObjectAttribute>,
74:     pub relationships: Vec<RelationshipDefinition>,
75: }
76: ```
77: 
78: The `id` attribute must exist and have a `Direct` mapping to the primary container's
79: primary key field. `DataObjectManager` uses it to locate records.
80: 
81: ### DataObjectAttribute
82: 
83: ```rust
84: pub struct DataObjectAttribute {
85:     pub name: String,
86:     pub data_type: ValueType,
87:     pub mapping: AttributeMapping,
88:     pub description: Option<String>,
89: }
90: ```
91: 
92: Validation rules are no longer defined on `DataObjectAttribute`. They are defined
93: separately in the validation addon and attached to a `DataObjectDefinition` by name.
94: See [spec/validation.md](validation.md).
95: 
96: ### AttributeMapping
97: 
98: ```rust
99: pub enum AttributeMapping {
100:     Direct {
101:         container_id: String,
102:         field_name: String,
103:     },
104:     Calculated {
105:         expression: String,
106:     },
107: }
108: ```
109: 
110: **Calculated expressions** are string templates using `{attribute_name}` substitution,
111: evaluated after all `Direct` attributes are loaded. Example: `"{first_name} {last_name}"`.
112: Calculated attributes are read-only and not written back on save.
113: 
114: ---
115: 
116: ## Relationships
117: 
118: ### RelationshipDefinition
119: 
120: ```rust
121: pub struct RelationshipDefinition {
122:     pub id: String,
123:     pub name: String,                         // human name, e.g. "orders"
124:     pub from_container_id: String,
125:     pub to_container_id: String,
126:     pub cardinality: Cardinality,
127:     pub join_type: JoinType,
128:     pub conditions: Vec<JoinCondition>,
129: }
130: ```
131: 
132: ### Cardinality
133: 
134: ```rust
135: pub enum Cardinality {
136:     OneToOne,
137:     OneToMany,
138:     ManyToMany { junction_container_id: String },
139: }
140: ```
141: 
142: `OneToOne` — a single matching row in the target container.
143: `OneToMany` — multiple rows in the target container; the GDO's attribute for this
144: relationship holds a `Vec` of child GDOs (loaded separately, not inlined in the
145: serializable map).
146: `ManyToMany` — a junction container maps source IDs to target IDs; both sides are loaded
147: as `Vec` of GDOs.
148: 
149: ### JoinType
150: 
151: ```rust
152: pub enum JoinType { Inner, Left, Right, Outer }
153: ```
154: 
155: ### JoinCondition
156: 
157: ```rust
158: pub struct JoinCondition {
159:     pub from_field: String,
160:     pub to_field: String,
161:     pub operator: String,   // "=" | "<" | ">" | "<=" | ">=" | "!="
162: }
163: ```
164: 
165: ---
166: 
167: ## DataObjectManager
168: 
169: ```rust
170: pub struct DataObjectManager {
171:     pub dictionary: DataDictionary,
172: }
173: 
174: impl DataObjectManager {
175:     pub fn new() -> Self;
176:     pub fn with_dictionary(dict: DataDictionary) -> Self;
177: }
178: ```
179: 
180: ### Manager Callback Events
181: 
182: All manager operations fire events through the GDO's two-level callback dispatch.
183: 
184: | Event | Fired by |
185: |-------|----------|
186: | `before_load` / `after_load` / `on_error_load` | `load_data_object` |
187: | `before_save` / `after_save` / `on_error_save` | `save_data_object` |
188: | `before_delete_object` / `after_delete_object` / `on_error_delete_object` | `delete_data_object` |
189: 
190: ### create_data_object
191: 
192: ```rust
193: pub fn create_data_object(&self, identifier_name: &str) -> GenericDataObject
194: ```
195: 
196: Returns a new GDO with a fresh UUID and state `DataObjectState::New`. No callbacks fired.
197: 
198: ### load_data_object
199: 
200: ```rust
201: pub fn load_data_object(&self, object_id: &str, id: &str) -> Result<GenericDataObject, String>
202: ```
203: 
204: 1. Fire `before_load` on the (not-yet-populated) GDO shell.
205: 2. Look up `DataObjectDefinition`. Build `QueryPlan` from attribute mappings and relationships.
206: 3. Execute plan via `QueryEngine`. Map result row to GDO attributes:
207:    - `Direct`: convert via `ConversionRegistry`.
208:    - `Calculated`: evaluate expression after `Direct` attributes are loaded.
209: 4. Set `PersistenceInfo` (driver + location from primary container). Set state `Hydrated`.
210: 5. Fire `after_load`. On failure: fire `on_error_load`.
211: 
212: ### save_data_object
213: 
214: ```rust
215: pub fn save_data_object(&self, object_id: &str, gdo: &mut GenericDataObject) -> Result<(), String>
216: ```
217: 
218: 1. Fire `before_save`.
219: 2. Look up `DataObjectDefinition`. Check transaction state on GDO.
220: 3. For each container touched by `Direct` mappings: build sub-map, call `driver.persist`.
221:    - **With active transaction:** if any write fails, undo completed writes, call
222:      `rollback_transaction()`, fire `on_error_save`, return `Err`.
223:    - **Without transaction:** log failures, continue, fire `after_save` with warning.
224: 4. On full success: update state `Consistent`. Fire `after_save`.
225: 
226: Calculated attributes are skipped. Validation must be run separately via the validation
227: addon before calling `save_data_object`.
228: 
229: ### delete_data_object
230: 
231: ```rust
232: pub fn delete_data_object(&self, object_id: &str, gdo: &mut GenericDataObject) -> Result<(), String>
233: ```
234: 
235: 1. Fire `before_delete_object`.
236: 2. Call `gdo.delete()` (marks state `Deleted`).
237: 3. Call `gdo.persist()` (calls `driver.delete()` on the backing store).
238: 4. Fire `after_delete_object`. On failure: fire `on_error_delete_object`.
239: 
240: ---
241: 
242: ## Crate Dependencies
243: 
244: | Crate | Purpose |
245: |-------|---------|
246: | `ox_data_object` | `GenericDataObject`, `AttributeValue` |
247: | `ox_persistence` | `PERSISTENCE_DRIVER_REGISTRY`, `DataObjectState` |
248: | `ox_type_converter` | `ValueType`, `CONVERSION_REGISTRY` |
249: | `ox_callback_manager` | `CALLBACK_MANAGER`, `EventType` |
250: | `serde` / `serde_json` | Dictionary serialization |
251: | `anyhow` | Error handling in query engine |
